Package com.salesforce.multicloudj.dbbackuprestore.driver

Class Restore

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.salesforce.multicloudj.dbbackuprestore.driver.Restore
  • public class Restore
extends Object
    Represents a database restore operation. Contains metadata about the restore including its status, source backup, and timing information.
    Since:
    0.2.25

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class Description
      static class  Restore.RestoreBuilder

      Creates a new Restore instance.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      static Restore.RestoreBuilder builder()  
      String getBackupId()
      ID of the backup being restored from.
      Instant getEndTime()
      Timestamp when the restore operation completed.
      String getRestoreId()
      Unique identifier for the restore operation.
      Instant getStartTime()
      Timestamp when the restore operation started.
      RestoreStatus getStatus()
      Current status of the restore operation.
      String getStatusMessage()
      Status or failure message from the restore job.
      String getTargetResource()
      Name of the target resource (table/database) being restored to.

      ID of the backup being restored from.
      void setEndTime​(Instant endTime)
      Timestamp when the restore operation completed.

      Unique identifier for the restore operation.
      void setStartTime​(Instant startTime)
      Timestamp when the restore operation started.

      Current status of the restore operation.

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Restore

        public Restore()

      • Restore

        public Restore​(String restoreId,
               String backupId,
               String targetResource,
               RestoreStatus status,
               Instant startTime,
               Instant endTime,
               String statusMessage)
        Creates a new Restore instance.
        Parameters:
        restoreId - Unique identifier for the restore operation. This ID can be used to track the progress of the restore.
        backupId - ID of the backup being restored from.
        targetResource - Name of the target resource (table/database) being restored to.
        status - Current status of the restore operation.
        startTime - Timestamp when the restore operation started.
        endTime - Timestamp when the restore operation completed. Will be null if the restore is still in progress.
        statusMessage - Status or failure message from the restore job. For failed jobs, this typically contains the provider-specific restore error reason.

        Timestamp when the restore operation completed. Will be null if the restore is still in progress.

        Status or failure message from the restore job. For failed jobs, this typically contains the provider-specific restore error reason.
